conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
# Pre-Game Reflection

I felt most connected to F, J, and G. F and I developed a genuine strategic partnership from the start—we talked through game theory together and agreed to scout the room methodically before showing our cards, which felt like real collaboration. J and I bonded over our shared family obligations and the weight of that responsibility, and there was something honest about that mutual understanding. G also connected with me on that family level, though I appreciated that he respected when I was direct about needing to do what's best for my family, even if it meant not cooperating. I felt least connected to A, honestly—the conversation was fine but felt more surface-level and less substantive than the others.

I didn't make any formal agreements, but F and I did agree to explore the room together and compare notes before the game starts, and I told several people (B, D, H, I) that I'd circle back to strategize once we understood the actual rules. I trust F the most right now—we've been straightforward with each other from the beginning and our approach feels aligned. I'm cautious about everyone else, honestly. I respect J, G, C, and H for their honesty about their situations, but I'm not sure yet who will actually cooperate versus compete when real money is on the line.
